---
title: "Chaffweed (Lysimachia minima)"
slug: "lysimachia-minima"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/622628690/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Chaffweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Ericales"
tax_family: "Primulaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Ericales", "Primulaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://static.inaturalist.org/photos/622628690/medium.jpg" alt="Chaffweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/stevecollins">
stevecollins</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341960961">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Ericales
-   **Family:** Primulaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=204353)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist, sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A tiny, low-growing wildflower characterized by its small, opposite leaves and minute white to pale pink flowers. In Florida, it is found in moist, sandy habitats such as flatwoods, wet prairies, and seasonally wet depressions. It grows during the warmer months and is often overlooked due to its small size and inconspicuous appearance.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/204353-Lysimachia-minima)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/927)
